name: Rewrite OAuth2 example to Hono + Drizzle
description: Rewrite the @jmondi/oauth2-server example server from NestJS/Express + Prisma to HonoJS + Drizzle, preserving grants, routes, and seed data.
why: The example is the reference integration for @jmondi/oauth2-server. NestJS/Express + Prisma is heavy, decorator-laden, and not Fetch-native. A Hono + Drizzle rewrite shows the library wired into a smaller, modern, Fetch-native stack and fixes latent bugs (dropped scopes, an unlinked seed) so the example teaches correct patterns.
outcome: A HonoJS server on @hono/node-server (port 3000, /api prefix) backed by Drizzle/postgres.js, bridging OAuth via the framework-agnostic vanilla Fetch adapter, with scopes correctly persisted and a Vitest integration suite proving the full authorization_code + PKCE and refresh_token flows. The untouched SvelteKit client keeps working.
in_scope: CSRF swap: csrf-csrf double-submit -> hono/csrf origin-based
Fix scope persistence via three explicit join tables and a fixed seed
Add a Vitest integration test suite against a dedicated test database
Update tooling (package.json, tsconfig, scripts), README, and CLAUDE.md
Modernize the root tsconfig.json + package.json to ESNext + strict ESM (NodeNext modules retained; web/ untouched)
out_of_scope: Adding new OAuth grants (client_credentials/password/implicit stay disabled)
Changing public /api routes, port 3000, or the seed IDs/values
Enforcing client-declared scopes in finalize() (modeled but not enforced)

use_cases.description: A developer starts the server and hits GET /api/ping for a health check.
A client app starts the authorization_code + PKCE flow at GET /api/oauth2/authorize; an unauthenticated user is redirected to the login form.
A user submits the login form; the server sets the jid session cookie and redirects back to authorize.
An authorized request completes and redirects to the client redirect_uri with code and state.
A client exchanges the auth code with its PKCE verifier at POST /api/oauth2/token for access + refresh tokens carrying the granted scopes.
A client uses the refresh_token grant to rotate tokens and narrow scopes to a subset of the originally granted scopes.
A client revokes a token at POST /api/oauth2/revoke.
A maintainer runs the Vitest suite against a dedicated test database to verify the full flow.

decisions: Runtime: Node + @hono/node-server; tsx watch replaces nodemon/ts-node; port 3000 and the /api prefix are preserved
OAuth HTTP is bridged via the framework-agnostic vanilla Fetch adapter (requestFromVanilla/responseToVanilla); no Hono adapter exists or is needed (ADR-0001)
CSRF via hono/csrf (origin-based); csrf-csrf and the hidden _csrf field removed
Templating via Hono JSX; nunjucks and views/ removed
ORM: Drizzle + postgres.js; inet columns use Drizzle native inet(); Prisma removed

Scopes are persisted: three explicit join tables wired in persist() within a transaction, getByIdentifier loads scopes, and the seed links client->scopes; finalize() stays verbatim, no enforcement (ADR-0001)
Grant set unchanged: authorization_code (PKCE/S256) + refresh_token; the seed client's stray client_credentials grant is left as-is
Tests: Vitest integration suite against a dedicated oauth_test database via .env.test + globalSetup
Implementation happens in a git worktree on branch hono-drizzle-rewrite

JWT signing secret moves to a JWT_SECRET env var (added to .env, .env.example, and tests/.env.test), replacing the hardcoded 'super-secret' in the old oauth.module.ts; MyCustomJwtService is constructed from process.env.JWT_SECRET and the currentUser middleware verifies with the same service. The existing .env.example (DATABASE_URL) is extended with JWT_SECRET

Resilience negative paths (OAuthException mapping, bad secret, expired code, revoked refresh, CSRF reject) live in a dedicated resilience slice that reuses the Vitest test-DB harness, rather than being folded into the integration flow slice

The Vitest + test-DB harness (vitest.config, tests/.env.test, globalSetup migrate+seed, between-test truncation) is its own slice upstream of the test-first repo/endpoint slices, so they have a harness to run against; the integration-flow and resilience slices reuse it unchanged

OAuth error mapping is hand-rolled in the route handlers: the vanilla adapter ships no handleExpressError equivalent, so OAuthException is caught and mapped to a Fetch response mirroring handleExpressError's body ({ status, message, error: errorType, error_description })

The root server tsconfig is modernized to ESNext + strict in issue 1: strict, verbatimModuleSyntax, isolatedModules, moduleDetection 'force', resolveJsonModule, skipLibCheck, lib [ESNext] - keeping target ESNext and module/moduleResolution NodeNext (correct for a Node/tsx server, NOT bundler). package.json keeps type:module, bumps @types/node, and adds an engines.node floor. web/ stays untouched (SvelteKit-managed, already strict). Cross-cutting: verbatimModuleSyntax forces `import type` for all type-only imports across the new code, and every slice must compile strict-clean - enforced when pnpm build (tsc) goes green in the cleanup slice (12)
risks: Persisting scopes and linking the seed are behavior changes vs the old example; flow tests must assert the new scope round-trip and refresh narrowing
The Drizzle migration must faithfully reproduce Postgres-specific types (uuid, inet, enum arrays) so existing seed IDs and the SvelteKit client keep working
Seed IDs, redirect URI http://localhost:5173/callback, and /api paths/port 3000 are hardcoded in web/; any drift breaks the client
pnpm-workspace.yaml allowBuilds currently allowlists nest/prisma/swc build scripts; stale entries must be pruned and esbuild (tsx's dep) added only if pnpm flags it

tsc (pnpm build) will not fully pass mid-chain while old NestJS files remain; dev (tsx) and tests (vitest) are transpile-only, so verification relies on them until the cleanup slice removes dead files

hono/csrf is origin-based and must be applied ONLY to the form POST routes (login/scopes), never to token/revoke (machine-to-machine, no browser Origin); the SvelteKit dev flow passes CSRF because Vite's proxy uses changeOrigin:false (Host stays localhost:5173 so Origin matches) - flipping changeOrigin to true would break the login POST. No hono/cors is needed because the browser is same-origin with the proxy.

DATABASE_URL carries a Prisma-style ?schema=public param and the old entrypoint used dotenv; verify postgres.js accepts the connection string (strip ?schema=public if it errors) and ensure the new src/index.ts and src/db/index.ts load .env (keep dotenv or use tsx --env-file)
validation: Full authorization_code + PKCE flow completes via Vitest app.request(): authorize -> login -> code -> token, with granted scopes present on the issued token
refresh_token grant rotates tokens and can narrow scopes to a subset
Manual SvelteKit login -> callback round trip succeeds against the rewritten server
No remaining @nestjs/*, @prisma/client, express, nunjucks, or class-validator imports; pnpm build (tsc) passes after cleanup
notes: Key modules: src/index.ts (composition root + serve), src/container.ts (db, repos, JWT, AuthorizationServer), src/db/{schema,index,seed}.ts + drizzle.config.ts, src/app/oauth/{entities,repositories} (5 each), route handlers for token/revoke/authorize, currentUser Hono middleware (hono/cookie + JWT, typed via Hono Variables), src/views/*.tsx (Hono JSX), tests/. OAuth methods: respondToAccessTokenRequest, revoke, validateAuthorizationRequest, completeAuthorizationRequest. authorize preserves the hardcoded isAuthorizationApproved=true (@todo). Scope persistence wires oauthClientScopes/oauthAuthCodeScopes/oauthTokenScopes transactionally. Test DB strategy: dedicated oauth_test DB via .env.test, globalSetup migrates+seeds, truncate dynamic tables (tokens, auth codes, their scope joins) between tests while keeping seeded users/clients/scopes.
